This is the most severe source crisis since tracking began.

escalatedRisks.status: CRITICAL — July 3, 2026 09:00。NEMIX DDR5-6400 32GB and DDR5-5600 32GB both OOS for second consecutive day。Newegg anti-bot block ('Are you a human?') makes independent verification impossible。69 days of continuous availability ended。If not restored by Day 3 (July 4), classify as structural (non-recoverable) retail anchor loss。
escalatedRisks.source: Newegg.com (last verified July 1, 2026; now anti-bot blocked)
escalatedRisks.detail: This is the most significant source health change in DDR5 tracking since Day 1。The 32GB DDR5-6400 NEMIX anchor at $1,488 has been verified 97 consecutive times over 69 days。Its OOS combined with Newegg anti-bot blockage creates a complete verification gap——we can neither confirm OOS persistence nor detect recovery。

escalatedRisks.risk: 🔴🔴 NEWEGG ANTI-BOT BLOCK — Newegg.com now blocking all automated access with 'Are you a human?' challenge。This was the primary DDR5 RDIMM retail anchor (NEMIX) and PCIe5 TLC retail anchor (9550 Pro)。
escalatedRisks.status: NEW — July 3, 2026 09:00。All Newegg URLs return 'Are you a human?' anti-bot challenge。This blocks: (1) NEMIX DDR5-6400 32GB OOS verification, (2) NEMIX DDR5-5600 32GB OOS verification, (3) Newegg 9550 Pro pricing, (4) Newegg QLC pricing, (5) A-Tech DDR5 pricing。This is potentially the most damaging single-source loss——Newegg was last remaining retail anchor for DDR5 RDIMM。
escalatedRisks.detail: Newegg was providing 5+ independent pricing anchors across DDR5, PCIe5 TLC, and PCIe4 QLC。Its loss makes DDR5 RDIMM retail tracking effectively impossible for the time being。CDW partially fills the SSD gap but cannot replace DDR5 RDIMM pricing。
